title: Telemetry Backend
description: Self-hosted observability backend combining Prometheus (metrics storage and alerting), Grafana (dashboards), and Loki (structured log aggregation). Provides the storage and query layer for traces, metrics, and logs; consumed by instrumentation and monitoring strategy patterns. Preferred when data residency, cost control, or customization requirements preclude a managed SaaS observability backend.

cost.costNotes: Self-hosted OSS stack (Prometheus, Grafana, Loki). Min ($30): single-node on small VM. Max ($500): production HA cluster with persistent storage. Commercial SaaS alternatives (Datadog, Grafana Cloud, AWS CloudWatch) will have higher and consumption-based costs.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Self-hosted Prometheus+Grafana+Loki on cloud VMs. Small (1-2 services): t3.small or equivalent ~$15-20/month compute + ~$10/month storage (EBS/PD). Production HA (10-50 services): 2-3 nodes for Prometheus HA + Loki ingester/querier cluster, 50-100GB persistent storage, ~$200-500/month. Does not include data egress costs or commercial support.
provenance.adoptionCost: Setup includes: (1) provisioning and configuring Prometheus scrape jobs (8-12 hours), (2) Loki + Promtail/OTEL log pipeline configuration (8-12 hours), (3) Grafana dashboard setup and alerting rules (8-16 hours), (4) persistent storage and retention configuration (4-8 hours). Total ~28-48 hours at $40-50/hour blended with AI assistance = ~$1400-2400.
provenance.licenseCost: Prometheus (Apache 2.0), Grafana (AGPL for OSS edition), Loki (AGPL) — all free to self-host. No licensing costs for the open-source stack.

provides.reasoning: This pattern IS the telemetry backend — it provides the storage and query infrastructure that instrumentation layers export to. Prometheus stores metrics; Loki stores logs; Grafana provides query and visualization.
Loki (part of the Grafana stack) ingests and indexes structured logs shipped via Promtail or the OpenTelemetry Collector. This enables log querying by label and correlation with metrics/traces.
Prometheus stores all scraped and pushed metrics as time-series data with configurable retention. Supports PromQL queries over time windows for SLI/SLO calculations and alerting.

The telemetry backend captures and stores discrete events (counter increments, gauge changes, log entries) emitted by applications, providing event-tracking infrastructure for compliance, debugging, and observability purposes.

supports_nfr.reason: Self-hosted telemetry backend requires additional HA configuration for 4-nines availability targets. Consider managed observability SaaS at this tier.
Telemetry backend is meaningful from 90% availability upward; below that, service reliability is the primary concern, not observability infrastructure.
Telemetry backend is compatible with or without formal audit logging requirements.
Self-hosted Prometheus+Loki restart and WAL replay typically takes 5-15 minutes; RTO targets of 15 minutes or greater are supported without HA mode.
Prometheus handles millions of time-series samples per hour; Loki scales horizontally for high log ingestion rates.
Prometheus scrapes metrics asynchronously on a pull model; it does not add per-request overhead to application read paths.
Loki log ingestion and Prometheus remote-write are asynchronous background operations that do not constrain application write throughput.
Self-hosted telemetry backend supports GDPR compliance through on-premises data residency and configurable retention policies.
Self-hosted Prometheus+Grafana+Loki can be deployed in HIPAA-compliant environments with proper access controls and encryption.
On-premises telemetry storage is compatible with CCPA requirements when metric labels and logs are configured to exclude personal data.
Self-hosted telemetry backend supports SOX audit requirements through configurable log retention and access logging.

supports_constraints.reason: Prometheus+Grafana+Loki runs on any cloud or on-premises infrastructure.
The telemetry backend is language-agnostic; it receives telemetry from any language via standard protocols (OTLP, Prometheus scrape, Loki push API).
A telemetry backend is applicable to any platform type that emits metrics, logs, or traces.

metrics_retention_days.description: How long Prometheus retains metrics data. Longer retention requires more disk. 15 days covers most incident lookback windows without excessive cost.

log_retention_days.description: How long Loki retains log data. Shorter retention reduces storage costs significantly; use tiering (ops-low-cost-observability) for longer compliance retention.

backend_ha.description: Deploy Prometheus and Loki in HA mode (multiple replicas). Increases cost and operational complexity but prevents observability blind spots during backend restarts.
